string.description: Primitive type for immutable UTF-16 character sequences

number.description: 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point, the only numeric type

boolean.description: Logical data type with exactly two values: true and false

bigint.description: Arbitrary-precision integer type for numbers beyond Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER

symbol.description: Unique and immutable identifier used as object property keys

undefined.description: Default value for uninitialized variables and missing properties

null.description: Intentional absence of object value, distinct from undefined in strict null checks

void.description: Return type for functions that do not return a value

never.description: Type representing values that never occur; for functions that always throw or infinite loops

unknown.description: Type-safe counterpart of any; requires type narrowing before use

any.description: Opts out of type checking; compatible with every type, should be avoided when possible

Array<T>.description: Generic ordered collection of typed elements with index access

Record<K,V>.description: Utility type constructing an object type with specified key type and value type

Partial<T>.description: Utility type making all properties of Type optional

Required<T>.description: Utility type making all properties of Type required

Pick<T,K>.description: Utility type creating a type by picking specific properties from Type

Omit<T,K>.description: Utility type creating a type by omitting specific properties from Type

Promise<T>.description: Represents eventual completion of an async operation with typed resolve value

Map<K,V>.description: Key-value collection maintaining insertion order with any key type

Set<T>.description: Collection of unique values with O(1) membership testing
